NKJV.1: The Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave Him to show His servants—things which must shortly take place. And He sent and signified it by His angel to His servant John,
NKJV.2: who bore witness to the word of God, and to the testimony of Jesus Christ, to all things that he saw.
NKJV.3: Blessed is he who reads and those who hear the words of this prophecy, and keep those things which are written in it; for the time is near.
NKJV.4: John, to the seven churches which are in Asia: Grace to you and peace from Him who is and who was and who is to come, and from the seven Spirits who are before His throne,
NKJV.5: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the firstborn from the dead, and the ruler over the kings of the earth. To Him who loved us and washed us from our sins in His own blood,
NKJV.6: and has made us kings and priests to His God and Father, to Him be glory and dominion forever and ever. Amen.
NKJV.7: Behold, He is coming with clouds, and every eye will see Him, even they who pierced Him. And all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of Him. Even so, Amen.
NKJV.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End,” says the Lord, “who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.”
NKJV.9: I, John, both your brother and companion in the tribulation and kingdom and patience of Jesus Christ, was on the island that is called Patmos for the word of God and for the testimony of Jesus Christ.
NKJV.10: I was in the Spirit on the Lord’s Day, and I heard behind me a loud voice, as of a trumpet,
NKJV.11: saying, “I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last,” and, “What you see, write in a book and send it to the seven churches which are in Asia: to Ephesus, to Smyrna, to Pergamos, to Thyatira, to Sardis, to Philadelphia, and to Laodicea.”
NKJV.12: Then I turned to see the voice that spoke with me. And having turned I saw seven golden lampstands,
NKJV.13: and in the midst of the seven lampstands One like the Son of Man, clothed with a garment down to the feet and girded about the chest with a golden band.
NKJV.14: His head and hair were white like wool, as white as snow, and His eyes like a flame of fire;
NKJV.15: His feet were like fine brass, as if refined in a furnace, and His voice as the sound of many waters;
NKJV.16: He had in His right hand seven stars, out of His mouth went a sharp two-edged sword, and His countenance was like the sun shining in its strength.
NKJV.17: And when I saw Him, I fell at His feet as dead. But He laid His right hand on me, saying to me, “Do not be afraid;  I am the First and the Last.
NKJV.18: I am He who lives, and was dead, and behold, I am alive forevermore. Amen. And I have the keys of Hades and of Death.
NKJV.19: Write the things which you have  seen,  and the things which are,  and the things which will take place after this.
NKJV.20: The mystery of the seven stars which you saw in My right hand, and the seven golden lampstands: The seven stars are  the angels of the seven churches, and  the seven lampstands which you saw are the seven churches.

ESV.1: The revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show to his servants  the things that must soon take place. He made it known by sending his angel to his servant John,
ESV.2: who bore witness to the word of God and to the testimony of Jesus Christ, even to all that he saw.
ESV.3: Blessed is the one who reads aloud the words of this prophecy, and blessed are those who hear, and who keep what is written in it, for the time is near.
ESV.4: John to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace to you and peace from him who is and who was and who is to come, and from the seven spirits who are before his throne,
ESV.5: and from Jesus Christ the faithful witness, the firstborn of the dead, and the ruler of kings on earth. To him who loves us and has freed us from our sins by his blood
ESV.6: and made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father, to him be glory and dominion forever and ever. Amen.
ESV.7: Behold, he is coming with the clouds, and every eye will see him, even those who pierced him, and all tribes of the earth will wail  on account of him. Even so. Amen.
ESV.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,” says the Lord God, “who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.”
ESV.9: I, John, your brother and partner in the tribulation and the kingdom and the patient endurance that are in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos on account of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus.
ESV.10: I was in the Spirit on the Lord's day, and I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet
ESV.11: saying,  “Write what you see in a book and send it to the seven churches, to Ephesus and to Smyrna and to Pergamum and to Thyatira and to Sardis and to Philadelphia and to Laodicea.”
ESV.12: Then I turned to see the voice that was speaking to me, and on turning I saw seven golden lampstands,
ESV.13: and in the midst of the lampstands one like a son of man, clothed with a long robe and with a golden sash around his chest.
ESV.14: The hairs of his head were white, like white wool, like snow. His eyes were like a flame of fire,
ESV.15: his feet were like burnished bronze, refined in a furnace, and his voice was like the roar of many waters.
ESV.16: In his right hand he held seven stars, from his mouth came a sharp two-edged sword, and his face was like the sun shining in full strength.
ESV.17: When I saw him, I fell at his feet as though dead. But he laid his right hand on me, saying, “Fear not, I am the first and the last,
ESV.18: and the living one. I died, and behold I am alive forevermore, and I have the keys of Death and Hades.
ESV.19: Write therefore the things that you have seen, those that are and those that are to take place after this.
ESV.20: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand, and the seven golden lampstands, the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.

NASB.1: The Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave Him to show to His bond-servants, the things which must soon take place; and He sent and communicated [it] by His angel to His bond-servant John,
NASB.2: who testified to the word of God and to the testimony of Jesus Christ, [even] to all that he saw.
NASB.3: Blessed is he who reads and those who hear the words of the prophecy, and heed the things which are written in it; for the time is near.
NASB.4: John to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace to you and peace, from Him who is and who was and who is to come, and from the seven Spirits who are before His throne,
NASB.5: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the firstborn of the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To Him who loves us and released us from our sins by His blood--
NASB.6: and He has made us [to be] a kingdom, priests to His God and Father-- to Him [be] the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
NASB.7: BEHOLD, HE IS COMING WITH THE CLOUDS, and every eye will see Him, even those who pierced Him; and all the tribes of the earth will mourn over Him. So it is to be. Amen.
NASB.8: 'I am the Alpha and the Omega,' says the Lord God, 'who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.'
NASB.9: I, John, your brother and fellow partaker in the tribulation and kingdom and perseverance [which are] in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus.
NASB.10: I was in the Spirit on the Lord's day, and I heard behind me a loud voice like [the sound] of a trumpet,
NASB.11: saying, 'Write in a book what you see, and send [it] to the seven churches: to Ephesus and to Smyrna and to Pergamum and to Thyatira and to Sardis and to Philadelphia and to Laodicea.'
NASB.12: Then I turned to see the voice that was speaking with me. And having turned I saw seven golden lampstands;
NASB.13: and in the middle of the lampstands [I saw] one like a son of man, clothed in a robe reaching to the feet, and girded across His chest with a golden sash.
NASB.14: His head and His hair were white like white wool, like snow; and His eyes were like a flame of fire.
NASB.15: His feet [were] like burnished bronze, when it has been made to glow in a furnace, and His voice [was] like the sound of many waters.
NASB.16: In His right hand He held seven stars, and out of His mouth came a sharp two-edged sword; and His face was like the sun shining in its strength.
NASB.17: When I saw Him, I fell at His feet like a dead man. And He placed His right hand on me, saying, 'Do not be afraid; I am the first and the last,
NASB.18: and the living One; and I was dead, and behold, I am alive forevermore, and I have the keys of death and of Hades.
NASB.19: 'Therefore write the things which you have seen, and the things which are, and the things which will take place after these things.
NASB.20: 'As for the mystery of the seven stars which you saw in My right hand, and the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.

NLT.1: This is a revelation from Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants the events that must soon take place. He sent an angel to present this revelation to his servant John,
NLT.2: who faithfully reported everything he saw. This is his report of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus Christ.
NLT.3: God blesses the one who reads the words of this prophecy to the church, and he blesses all who listen to its message and obey what it says, for the time is near.
NLT.4: This letter is from John to the seven churches in the province of Asia. Grace and peace to you from the one who is, who always was, and who is still to come; from the sevenfold Spirit before his throne;
NLT.5: and from Jesus Christ. He is the faithful witness to these things, the first to rise from the dead, and the ruler of all the kings of the world. All glory to him who loves us and has freed us from our sins by shedding his blood for us.
NLT.6: He has made us a Kingdom of priests for God his Father. All glory and power to him forever and ever! Amen.
NLT.7:
Look! He comes with the clouds of heaven.
And everyone will see him —
even those who pierced him.
And all the nations of the world
will mourn for him.
Yes! Amen!
NLT.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega — the beginning and the end,” says the Lord God. “I am the one who is, who always was, and who is still to come — the Almighty One.”
NLT.9: I, John, am your brother and your partner in suffering and in God’s Kingdom and in the patient endurance to which Jesus calls us. I was exiled to the island of Patmos for preaching the word of God and for my testimony about Jesus.
NLT.10: It was the Lord’s Day, and I was worshiping in the Spirit. Suddenly, I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet blast.
NLT.11: It said, “Write in a book everything you see, and send it to the seven churches in the cities of Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
NLT.12: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
NLT.13: And standing in the middle of the lampstands was someone like the Son of Man. He was wearing a long robe with a gold sash across his chest.
NLT.14: His head and his hair were white like wool, as white as snow. And his eyes were like flames of fire.
NLT.15: His feet were like polished bronze refined in a furnace, and his voice thundered like mighty ocean waves.
NLT.16: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp two-edged sword came from his mouth. And his face was like the sun in all its brilliance.
NLT.17: When I saw him, I fell at his feet as if I were dead. But he laid his right hand on me and said, “Don’t be afraid! I am the First and the Last.
NLT.18: I am the living one. I died, but look — I am alive forever and ever! And I hold the keys of death and the grave.
NLT.19: “Write down what you have seen — both the things that are now happening and the things that will happen.
NLT.20: This is the meaning of the mystery of the seven stars you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands: The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.

WEB.1: This is the Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show to his servants the things which must happen soon, which he sent and made known by his angel  to his servant, John,
WEB.2: who testified to God’s word, and of the testimony of Jesus Christ, about everything that he saw.
WEB.3: Blessed is he who reads and those who hear the words of the prophecy, and keep the things that are written in it, for the time is at hand.
WEB.4: John, to the seven assemblies that are in Asia: Grace to you and peace, from God, who is and who was and who is to come; and from the seven Spirits who are before his throne;
WEB.5: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the firstborn of the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To him who loves us, and washed us from our sins by his blood;
WEB.6: and he made us to be a Kingdom, priests  to his God and Father; to him be the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
WEB.7: Behold,  he is coming with the clouds, and every eye will see him, including those who pierced him. All the tribes of the earth will mourn over him. Even so, Amen.
WEB.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,  ” says the Lord God,  “who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.”
WEB.9: I John, your brother and partner with you in the oppression, Kingdom, and perseverance in Christ Jesus, was on the isle that is called Patmos because of God’s Word and the testimony of Jesus Christ.
WEB.10: I was in the Spirit on the Lord’s day, and I heard behind me a loud voice, like a trumpet
WEB.11: saying, “What you see, write in a book and send to the seven assemblies:  to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and to Laodicea.”
WEB.12: I turned to see the voice that spoke with me. Having turned, I saw seven golden lamp stands.
WEB.13: And among the lamp stands was one like a son of man,  clothed with a robe reaching down to his feet, and with a golden sash around his chest.
WEB.14: His head and his hair were white as white wool, like snow. His eyes were like a flame of fire.
WEB.15: His feet were like burnished brass, as if it had been refined in a furnace. His voice was like the voice of many waters.
WEB.16: He had seven stars in his right hand. Out of his mouth proceeded a sharp two-edged sword. His face was like the sun shining at its brightest.
WEB.17: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. He laid his right hand on me, saying, “Don’t be afraid. I am the first and the last,
WEB.18: and the Living one. I was dead, and behold, I am alive forever more. Amen. I have the keys of Death and of Hades.
WEB.19: Write therefore the things which you have seen, and the things which are, and the things which will happen hereafter;
WEB.20: the mystery of the seven stars which you saw in my right hand, and the seven golden lamp stands. The seven stars are the angels  of the seven assemblies. The seven lamp stands are seven assemblies.

CSB17.1: The revelation of <sup>[1]</sup> Jesus Christ that God gave him to show his servants <sup>ⓐ</sup> what must soon take place. <sup>ⓑ</sup> He made it known by sending his angel <sup>ⓒ</sup> to his servant John, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.2: who testified to the word of God and to the testimony <sup>[2]</sup> of Jesus Christ, <sup>ⓔ</sup> whatever he saw. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.3: Blessed is the one who reads aloud the words of this prophecy, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and blessed are those who hear the words of this prophecy and keep <sup>[4]</sup> what is written in it, because the time is near.
CSB17.4: John: To the seven churches in Asia. Grace and peace <sup>ⓖ</sup> to you from <sup>[5]</sup> the one who is, who was, and who is to come, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and from the seven spirits <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> before his throne,
CSB17.5: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, <sup>ⓙ</sup> the firstborn from the dead <sup>ⓚ</sup> and the ruler of the kings of the earth. <sup>ⓛ</sup> To him who loves us and has set us free <sup>[7]</sup> from our sins by his blood, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.6: and made us a kingdom, <sup>[8]</sup> priests <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> to his God and Father — to him be glory and dominion forever and ever. Amen. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.7:
Look, he is coming with the clouds, ⓑ
and every eye will see him,
even those who pierced him.
And all the tribes [10] of the earth ⓒ ⓓ
will mourn over him. [11] ⓔ ⓕ
So it is to be. Amen.
CSB17.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,” <sup>ⓖ</sup> says the Lord God, “the one who is, who was, and who is to come, the Almighty.”
CSB17.9: I, John, your brother and partner in the affliction, <sup>ⓗ</sup> kingdom, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and endurance <sup>ⓙ</sup> that are in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.10: I was in the Spirit <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup> on the Lord’s day, and I heard a loud voice behind me like a trumpet <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.11: saying, “Write <sup>ⓝ</sup>on a scroll <sup>[13]</sup>what you see and send it to the seven churches: Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
CSB17.12: Then I turned to see whose voice <sup>ⓐ</sup> it was that spoke to me. When I turned I saw seven golden lampstands, <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.13: and among the lampstands was one like the Son of Man, <sup>[14]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup> dressed in a robe <sup>ⓓ</sup> and with a golden sash wrapped around his chest. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.14: The hair of his head was white as wool — white as snow <sup>ⓕ</sup>  — and his eyes like a fiery flame. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.15: His feet were like fine bronze as it is fired in a furnace, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and his voice like the sound of cascading <sup>[15]</sup> waters. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.16: He had seven stars in his right hand; a sharp double-edged sword came from his mouth, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and his face was shining like the sun at full strength. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.17: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. <sup>ⓛ</sup> He laid his right hand on me and said, “Don’t be afraid. I am the First and the Last, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.18: and the Living One. <sup>ⓝ</sup>I was dead, but look — I am alive forever and ever, <sup>ⓐ</sup>and I hold the keys <sup>ⓑ</sup>of death and Hades.
CSB17.19: Therefore write what you have seen, <sup>ⓒ</sup>what is, and what will take place after this.
CSB17.20: The mystery <sup>ⓓ</sup>of the seven stars you saw in my right hand and of the seven golden lampstands is this: The seven stars are the angels <sup>[16]</sup>of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands <sup>[17]</sup>are the seven churches. <sup>ⓔ</sup>

AMP.1: This is the revelation of Jesus Christ [His unveiling of the divine mysteries], which God [the Father] gave to Him to show to His bond-servants (believers) the things which must soon take place [in their entirety]; and He sent and communicated it by His angel (divine messenger) to His bond-servant John,
AMP.2: who testified and gave supporting evidence to the word of God and to the testimony of Jesus Christ, even to everything that he saw [in his visions].
AMP.3: Blessed (happy, prosperous, to be admired) is he who reads and those who hear the words of the prophecy, and who keep the things which are written in it [heeding them and taking them to heart]; for the time [of fulfillment] is near.
AMP.4: John, to the seven churches that are in [the province of] Asia: Grace [be granted] to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being], from Him Who is [existing forever] and Who was [continually existing in the past] and Who is to come, and from the seven Spirits that are before His throne,
AMP.5: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful and trustworthy Witness, the Firstborn of the dead, and the Ruler of the kings of the earth. To Him who [always] loves us and who [has once for all] freed us [or washed us] from our sins by His own blood (His sacrificial death)—
AMP.6: and formed us into a kingdom [as His subjects], priests to His God and Father—to Him be the glory and the power and the majesty and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
AMP.7: Behold, He is coming with the clouds, and every eye will see Him, even those who pierced Him; and all the tribes (nations) of the earth will mourn over Him [realizing their sin and guilt, and anticipating the coming wrath]. So it is to be. Amen.
AMP.8: "I am the Alpha and the Omega [the Beginning and the End]," says the Lord God, "Who is [existing forever] and Who was [continually existing in the past] and Who is to come, the Almighty [the Omnipotent, the Ruler of all]."
AMP.9: I, John, your brother and companion in the tribulation and kingdom and patient endurance which are in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos, [exiled there] because of [my preaching of] the word of God [regarding eternal salvation] and the testimony of Jesus Christ.
AMP.10: I was in the Spirit [in special communication with the Holy Spirit and empowered to receive and record the revelation from Jesus Christ] on the Lord's Day, and I heard behind me a loud voice like the sound of a trumpet,
AMP.11: saying, "Write on a scroll what you see [in this revelation], and send it to the seven churches—to Ephesus and to Smyrna and to Pergamum and to Thyatira and to Sardis and to Philadelphia and to Laodicea."
AMP.12: Then I turned to see the voice that was speaking with me. And after turning I saw seven golden lampstands;
AMP.13: and in the midst of the lampstands I saw someone like the Son of Man, dressed in a robe reaching to His feet, and with a golden sash wrapped around His chest.
AMP.14: His head and His hair were white like white wool, [glistening white] like snow; and His [all-seeing] eyes were [flashing] like a flame of fire [piercing into my being].
AMP.15: His feet were like burnished [white-hot] bronze, refined in a furnace, and His voice was [powerful] like the sound of many waters.
AMP.16: In His right hand He held seven stars, and from His mouth came a sharp two-edged sword [of judgment]; and His face [reflecting His majesty and the Shekinah glory] was like the sun shining in [all] its power [at midday].
AMP.17: When I saw Him, I fell at His feet as though dead. And He placed His right hand on me and said, "Do not be afraid; I am the First and the Last [absolute Deity, the Son of God],
AMP.18: and the Ever-living One [living in and beyond all time and space]. I died, but see, I am alive forevermore, and I have the keys of [absolute control and victory over] death and of Hades (the realm of the dead).
AMP.19: So write the things which you have seen [in the vision], and the things which are [now happening], and the things which will take place after these things.
AMP.20: As for the mystery of the seven stars which you saw in My right hand, and the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars are the angels ( divine messengers) of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.

MSG.1: A revealing of Jesus, the Messiah. God gave it to make plain to his servants what is about to happen. He published and delivered it by Angel to his servant John.
MSG.2: And John told everything he saw: God's Word—the witness of Jesus Christ!
MSG.3: How blessed the reader! How blessed the hearers and keepers of these oracle words, all the words written in this book! Time is just about up.
MSG.4: I, John, am writing this to the seven churches in Asia province: All the best to you from The God Who Is, The God Who Was, and The God About to Arrive, and from the Seven Spirits assembled before his throne,
MSG.5: and from Jesus Christ—Loyal Witness, Firstborn from the dead, Ruler of all earthly kings. Glory and strength to Christ, who loves us, who blood-washed our sins from our lives,
MSG.6: Who made us a Kingdom, Priests for his Father, forever—and yes, he's on his way!
MSG.7: Riding the clouds, he'll be seen by every eye, those who mocked and killed him will see him, People from all nations and all times will tear their clothes in lament. Oh, Yes.
MSG.8: The Master declares, "I'm A to Z. I'm The God Who Is, The God Who Was, and The God About to Arrive. I'm the Sovereign-Strong."
MSG.9: I, John, with you all the way in the trial and the Kingdom and the passion of patience in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of God's Word, the witness of Jesus.
MSG.10: It was Sunday and I was in the Spirit, praying. I heard a loud voice behind me, trumpet-clear and piercing:
MSG.11: "Write what you see into a book. Send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, Laodicea."
MSG.12: I turned and saw the voice. I saw a gold menorah with seven branches,
MSG.13: And in the center, the Son of Man, in a robe and gold breastplate,
MSG.14: hair a blizzard of white, Eyes pouring fire-blaze,
MSG.15: both feet furnace-fired bronze, His voice a cataract,
MSG.16: right hand holding the Seven Stars, His mouth a sharp-biting sword, his face a perigee sun.
MSG.17: I saw this and fainted dead at his feet. His right hand pulled me upright, his voice reassured me: "Don't fear: I am First, I am Last,
MSG.18: I'm Alive. I died, but I came to life, and my life is now forever. See these keys in my hand? They open and lock Death's doors, they open and lock Hell's gates.
MSG.19: Now write down everything you see: things that are, things about to be.
MSG.20: The Seven Stars you saw in my right hand and the seven-branched gold menorah—do you want to know what's behind them? The Seven Stars are the Angels of the seven churches; the menorah's seven branches are the seven churches."

NIV.1:
Prologue
The revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place. He made it known by sending his angel to his servant John,
NIV.2: who testifies to everything he saw—that is, the word of God and the testimony of Jesus Christ.
NIV.3: Blessed is the one who reads the words of this prophecy, and blessed are those who hear it and take to heart what is written in it, because the time is near.
NIV.4:
Greetings and Doxology
John,
To the seven churches in the province of Asia:
Grace and peace to you from him who is, and who was, and who is to come, and from the seven spirits before his throne,
NIV.5:
and from Jesus Christ, who is the faithful witness, the firstborn from the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth.
To him who loves us and has freed us from our sins by his blood,
NIV.6: and has made us to be a kingdom and priests to serve his God and Father—to him be glory and power for ever and ever! Amen.
NIV.7:
Look, he is coming with the clouds,
and every eye will see him,
even those who pierced him;
and all the peoples of the earth will mourn because of him.
So shall it be! Amen.
NIV.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,” says the Lord God, “who is, and who was, and who is to come, the Almighty.”
NIV.9:
One Like a Son of Man
I, John, your brother and companion in the suffering and kingdom and patient endurance that are ours in Jesus, was on the island of Patmos because of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus.
NIV.10: On the Lord’s Day I was in the Spirit, and I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet,
NIV.11: which said: “Write on a scroll what you see and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia and Laodicea.”
NIV.12: I turned around to see the voice that was speaking to me. And when I turned I saw seven golden lampstands,
NIV.13: and among the lampstands was someone “like a son of man,” dressed in a robe reaching down to his feet and with a golden sash around his chest.
NIV.14: His head and hair were white like wool, as white as snow, and his eyes were like blazing fire.
NIV.15: His feet were like bronze glowing in a furnace, and his voice was like the sound of rushing waters.
NIV.16: In his right hand he held seven stars, and out of his mouth came a sharp double-edged sword. His face was like the sun shining in all its brilliance.
NIV.17: When I saw him, I fell at his feet as though dead. Then he placed his right hand on me and said: “Do not be afraid. I am the First and the Last.
NIV.18: I am the Living One; I was dead, and behold I am alive for ever and ever! And I hold the keys of death and Hades.
NIV.19: “Write, therefore, what you have seen, what is now and what will take place later.
NIV.20: The mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and of the seven golden lampstands is this: The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.

NRSVCE.1: The revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place; he made it known by sending his angel to his servant John,
NRSVCE.2: who testified to the word of God and to the testimony of Jesus Christ, even to all that he saw.
NRSVCE.3: Blessed is the one who reads aloud the words of the prophecy, and blessed are those who hear and who keep what is written in it; for the time is near.
NRSVCE.4: John to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace to you and peace from him who is and who was and who is to come, and from the seven spirits who are before his throne,
NRSVCE.5: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the firstborn of the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To him who loves us and freed us from our sins by his blood,
NRSVCE.6: and made us to be a kingdom, priests serving his God and Father, to him be glory and dominion forever and ever. Amen.
NRSVCE.7:
Look! He is coming with the clouds;
every eye will see him,
even those who pierced him;
and on his account all the tribes of the earth will wail.
So it is to be. Amen.
NRSVCE.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,” says the Lord God, who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.
NRSVCE.9: I, John, your brother who share with you in Jesus the persecution and the kingdom and the patient endurance, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus.
NRSVCE.10: I was in the spirit on the Lord’s day, and I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet
NRSVCE.11: saying, “Write in a book what you see and send it to the seven churches, to Ephesus, to Smyrna, to Pergamum, to Thyatira, to Sardis, to Philadelphia, and to Laodicea.”
NRSVCE.12: Then I turned to see whose voice it was that spoke to me, and on turning I saw seven golden lampstands,
NRSVCE.13: and in the midst of the lampstands I saw one like the Son of Man, clothed with a long robe and with a golden sash across his chest.
NRSVCE.14: His head and his hair were white as white wool, white as snow; his eyes were like a flame of fire,
NRSVCE.15: his feet were like burnished bronze, refined as in a furnace, and his voice was like the sound of many waters.
NRSVCE.16: In his right hand he held seven stars, and from his mouth came a sharp, two-edged sword, and his face was like the sun shining with full force.
NRSVCE.17: When I saw him, I fell at his feet as though dead. But he placed his right hand on me, saying, “Do not be afraid; I am the first and the last,
NRSVCE.18: and the living one. I was dead, and see, I am alive forever and ever; and I have the keys of Death and of Hades.
NRSVCE.19: Now write what you have seen, what is, and what is to take place after this.
NRSVCE.20: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand, and the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.

NET.1: The revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must happen very soon. He made it clear by sending his angel to his servant John,
NET.2: who then testified to everything that he saw concerning the word of God and the testimony about Jesus Christ.
NET.3: Blessed is the one who reads the words of this prophecy aloud, and blessed are those who hear and obey the things written in it, because the time is near!
NET.4: From John, to the seven churches that are in the province of Asia: Grace and peace to you from “he who is,” and who was, and who is still to come, and from the seven spirits who are before his throne,
NET.5: and from Jesus Christ — the faithful witness, the firstborn from among the dead, the ruler over the kings of the earth. To the one who loves us and has set us free from our sins at the cost of his own blood
NET.6: and has appointed us as a kingdom, as priests serving his God and Father — to him be the glory and the power for ever and ever! Amen.
NET.7: (Look! He is returning with the clouds, and every eye will see him, even those who pierced him, and all the tribes on the earth will mourn because of him. This will certainly come to pass! Amen.)
NET.8: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,” says the Lord God — the one who is, and who was, and who is still to come — the All-Powerful!
NET.9: I, John, your brother and the one who shares with you in the persecution, kingdom, and endurance that are in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and the testimony about Jesus.
NET.10: I was in the Spirit on the Lord’s Day when I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet,
NET.11: saying: “Write in a book what you see and send it to the seven churches — to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
NET.12: I turned to see whose voice was speaking to me, and when I did so, I saw seven golden lampstands,
NET.13: and in the midst of the lampstands was one like a son of man. He was dressed in a robe extending down to his feet and he wore a wide golden belt around his chest.
NET.14: His head and hair were as white as wool, even as white as snow, and his eyes were like a fiery flame.
NET.15: His feet were like polished bronze refined in a furnace, and his voice was like the roar of many waters.
NET.16: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword extended out of his mouth. His face shone like the sun shining at full strength.
NET.17: When I saw him I fell down at his feet as though I were dead, but he placed his right hand on me and said: “Do not be afraid! I am the first and the last,
NET.18: and the one who lives! I was dead, but look, now I am alive — forever and ever — and I hold the keys of death and of Hades!
NET.19: Therefore write what you saw, what is, and what will be after these things.
NET.20: The mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and the seven golden lampstands is this: The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
